title: PMT Foundation, Bug Tracking, and Agile Workflow

sections.title: Project and Sprint Planning
items: Manage multiple projects with codes, team members, schedules, links, icons, and completion progress.
Organize work into Sprints with assigned teams, dates, lessons learned, and automatic progress calculations.
Finish a Sprint and carry unfinished work into the next Sprint, with direct navigation between projects, Sprints, and tasks.

sections.title: Dev Tasks and Kanban
items: Create Dev Tasks with rich descriptions, attachments, priorities, dependencies, Subtasks, assignments, schedules, URLs, statuses, and completion percentages.
Automatically calculate Parent Task progress from Subtasks and keep Project and Sprint totals current.
Filter, duplicate, reorder, and move work through the Kanban Board using drag and drop.

sections.title: QA and Bug Tracking
items: Introduce dedicated Bug Tracking with severity, environment, reporters, assignees, reproduction details, attachments, and shared workflow statuses.
Add a QA role with separate permissions for Bug Tracking and Dev Task work.
Create linked Dev Tasks when bugs are assigned to developers and include bugs in Project and Sprint progress.

sections.title: Scrum, Documentation, and Backlog
items: Provide shared daily Scrum entries with rich text, newest-first display, duplication, and pinning for important updates.
Add long-form Documentation with rich formatting, images or attachments, ownership controls, and edit history.
Introduce a Backlog for unplanned Dev Tasks and bugs, including Sprint visibility and carryover behavior.

sections.title: User Experience and Administration
items: Add dashboards for Project, Sprint, task, and bug progress with direct navigation into related work.
Modernize the interface with themes, responsive layouts, avatars, icon-based actions, tooltips, and PMT-styled dialogs.
Provide user profiles, password changes, role-based access, configurable lookup values, and rich read-only work-item details.

title: Dedicated QA Bug Tracking and Richer Task Details

sections.title: Dedicated Bug Tracking
items: Add a separate Bug Tracking workspace with severity, priority, environment, reporters, assignees, reproduction steps, expected results, actual results, and attachments.
Move bugs through the same Kanban workflow as Dev Tasks and include them in Project and Sprint completion totals.
Automatically create a related Dev Task when a bug is assigned to a developer.

sections.title: Role-Based Workflows
items: Introduce a QA role for creating and managing bug reports.
Prevent QA users from modifying Dev Tasks and developers from directly modifying QA-owned bug work.
Preserve unrestricted access for administrators.

sections.title: Improved Task Viewing
items: Open tasks in a dedicated read-only dialog before editing.
Render rich descriptions, pasted images, links, and attachments in a user-friendly format.
Open linked content and attachments in a separate browser tab.

sections.title: Configurable Dropdown Values
items: Centralize dropdown choices in administrator-maintained lookup lists.
Allow lookup values to be added, edited, deactivated, and reordered.
Hide inactive choices from new selections while continuing to display them correctly on older records.

title: Backlog Planning, Scrum Improvements, and Connected Navigation

sections.title: Backlog and Sprint Completion
items: Add a Backlog for creating and managing unscheduled Dev Tasks and bugs.
Show whether each Backlog item is already assigned to a Sprint.
Let users choose whether unfinished work should carry into the next Sprint.

sections.title: Daily Scrum and Documentation
items: Rename Dev Logs to Scrum and provide a guided daily update template covering accomplishments, plans, and roadblocks.
Rename Blogs to Documentation and open existing documents in read-only mode by default.
Scale pasted images to fit rich-text editors and read-only content areas.

sections.title: Dashboard Navigation
items: Show task titles and progress details within Project and Sprint dashboard views.
Navigate from dashboard Sprints and tasks to the corresponding filtered PMT screens.
Add clear graph legends and expandable detail controls.

sections.title: Assignment Accuracy
items: Limit task and bug assignees to members of the relevant Project or Sprint.
Continue allowing any PMT user to be selected as a bug reporter.
Provide consistent profile images and avatar display for seeded users.

title: Visual Scheduling with Gantt Charts and Road Maps

sections.title: Gantt Chart
items: Add a Project-based Gantt view with year, quarter, month, and day timelines.
Display Sprints, task schedules, assignments, completion, dependencies, and related bug status.
Open related Dev Tasks, bugs, and Sprint work directly from the chart.

sections.title: Flexible Scheduling
items: Add optional start and end dates to Projects, Sprints, Dev Tasks, and bugs.
Support both normal Sprints and long-running, multi-year Projects.
Omit weekends and configured holidays unless work is specifically scheduled on those dates.

sections.title: Road Map
items: Add a Road Map showing Projects and Sprints across a shared timeline.
Display completion progress, participants, and optional date details with filtering and sorting.
Navigate directly from Road Map items to their associated work.

sections.title: Holidays and Status Colors
items: Add Holiday maintenance suitable for teams working in different countries.
Make status and progress colors configurable through Settings.
Apply configured colors consistently throughout PMT charts and status indicators.

title: Task Audit History and More Reliable Dev–QA Workflows

sections.title: Task and Bug Audit History
items: Record who changed a Dev Task or bug, when it changed, and the previous and new status and completion values.
Add an Audit action to read-only and edit dialogs.
Present audit entries from newest to oldest.

sections.title: Workflow Automation
items: Preserve existing progress when work returns to Todo.
Keep Sprint assignment and Backlog transitions consistent.
Synchronize linked Dev Task and bug completion through development and QA transitions.

sections.title: Gantt and Road Map Focus
items: Add a Gantt Sprint selector that defaults to the current Sprint and positions the chart automatically.
Keep selected tasks visible beside the fixed Sprint-name column.
Allow Road Map users to hide Sprint rows and compare Project schedules directly.

title: Interactive Gantt Navigation and Compact Road Maps

sections.title: Gantt Controls
items: Sort Sprints and jump to a selected Sprint in focused or complete-project views.
Toggle weekends and holidays and reset the chart to the current Sprint.
Keep selectors and display controls synchronized.

sections.title: Sprint Fly-By
items: Add an animated Sprint fly-by for demonstrating Project history.
Start, pause, resume, stop, and reset the tour without losing the current viewport.
Pause at each Sprint so users can inspect its work.

sections.title: Reliability and Road Map Improvements
items: Restore correct timeline labels and keep the viewport aligned with the selected Sprint.
Compress unused Road Map space and prevent month-label overlap or clipping.
Enrich Project tooltips with completion and schedule information.

sections.title: Navigation
items: Make the browser Back button work consistently across PMT screens.

title: Streamlined Navigation and Richer Progress Views

sections.title: Navigation and Settings
items: Add icons and labels to top navigation and move overflow items into a responsive menu.
Rename key work areas for clarity and group administration tools in Settings.
Replace the user-name area with an avatar menu for account actions.

sections.title: Progress at a Glance
items: Redesign Project, Sprint, and Dashboard progress with active-status bars and an overall completion bar.
Hide zero-value categories and expand or collapse detailed cards.
Show Sprint tasks and their progress in scrollable Dashboard details.

sections.title: Consistent Interaction Design
items: Prevent controls from shifting when buttons become active.
Standardize icon-and-text page actions and icon-only card or table actions.
Show record titles in Dev Task and Bug edit-dialog captions.

sections.title: Drag-and-Drop Ordering
items: Reorder Dev Tasks and bugs within lists, Backlog groups, Sprints, and Kanban columns.
Display a clear insertion indicator while dragging.
Keep repeated drag-and-drop operations working without a refresh.

title: Light and Dark Themes with Interactive Bug Analytics

sections.title: Dev–QA Completion Safeguard
items: Prevent a linked Dev Task from reaching 100% until its bug has passed QA.
Explain the restriction clearly and complete the Dev Task automatically after QA passes the bug.

sections.title: Light and Dark Themes
items: Add a theme toggle to the user avatar menu and remember the user's choice.
Apply theme changes immediately and restore them without startup flicker.
Provide polished, readable light and dark treatments across PMT.

sections.title: Bug Tracking Analytics
items: Add charts for bug trends, severity, reported-versus-resolved totals, and the current Sprint mix.
Support chart drill-down and full-screen expansion.
Preserve drill-down lists while users inspect individual records.

sections.title: Documentation and Test Preferences
items: Add an All Projects Documentation filter and relevant imagery for seeded documents.
Add a Settings action to clear saved browser preferences for first-time-experience testing.
Rename Bug Reports to Bug Tracking.

title: Dev Task Analytics and More Efficient Task Editing

sections.title: Dev Task Filters and Editing
items: Add priority filtering and a dedicated show or hide filter control.
Display assignee avatars beside names and keep long selection lists scrollable.
Move attachments above assignee selection for a clearer editing flow.

sections.title: Bug Tracking Layout
items: Move Reporter and Assignee to the first columns and enlarge their avatars.
Show participant avatars in Bug dialogs and keep long lists usable.
Move bug attachments above reporter selection.

sections.title: Dev Task Visual Analytics
items: Add interactive charts for open and completed work in the current Sprint.
Compare Dev Task totals and completions across the latest six Sprints.
Visualize development statuses and workload distribution with chart drill-down.

sections.title: Visual Consistency
items: Apply consistent spacing between filter panels and content tables.
Improve demonstration data so completed Sprints and deployment states produce realistic charts.

title: Paper Design, Configurable Navigation, and Team Scheduling

sections.title: Scrum and Daily Work
items: Add Scrum filtering by Project, person, and date, with filters hidden until needed.
Open Scrum rows in read-only details and remember the user's last selected Project.
Stabilize pinned-entry interactions and remove unused flag visuals.

sections.title: Navigation and WFH Scheduling
items: Add configurable top navigation with show, hide, rename, drag-to-reorder, overflow synchronization, and reset controls.
Introduce WFH Schedule with weekday toggles, team ordering, hidden-user management, and reset functionality.
Move Settings into the main navigation and correct menu layering.

sections.title: Dev Tasks and Bugs
items: Make task and bug assignees optional and improve required-field validation and focus behavior.
Remember recent Project, Sprint, and Environment choices across work-entry screens.
Ensure Dev Tasks generated from bugs retain their Project and Sprint and can be opened from either linked record.

sections.title: Stable Paper Design
items: Establish no-layout-shift interactions for navigation, cards, controls, dialogs, and scrollbars.
Mute inactive controls and emphasize actions on hover.
Standardize solid semantic colors, borderless page actions, and clickable chart drill-down rows.

title: Unified Work Screens and the PMT Paper Design

sections.title: Dev Tasks and Bug Tracking
items: Refine Dev Task rows, Subtask labels, action icons, completion spacing, edit-mode reordering, and dialog titles.
Redesign filters with balanced layouts, avatars, aligned checkboxes, and consistent chart subtitles.
Bring Bug Tracking in line with Dev Tasks while preserving its specialized fields.

sections.title: Paper Design and Themes
items: Standardize title colors, monochrome icons, borderless buttons, hover-only emphasis, and icon sizing.
Improve dark-theme selected states and introduce a flatter white light theme.
Prevent dialogs and browser scrollbars from shifting background content.

sections.title: Branding and About
items: Introduce updated PMT branding and make the logo and title navigate to About.
Add an About page with the PMT logo, creator credit, website, and public repository link.
Keep navigation full width while confining scrollbars to page content.

sections.title: Scrum, Backlog, and WFH Schedule
items: Apply Dev Task interaction and visual conventions to Scrum and Backlog.
Ensure non-admin users can modify only their own Scrum entries.
Limit non-admin users to their own WFH schedules while reserving administrative controls for admins.

title: Richer Team Profiles, Unified Work Items, and Smarter Progress

sections.title: Users and Team Selection
items: Redesign user cards with larger avatars, clearer contact details, email links, and full-size image previews.
Add generic avatar selection and uploads with avatar and name requirements.
Reuse richer member selections across Projects, Sprints, work items, and WFH Schedule.

sections.title: Project and Sprint Insights
items: Add status donut charts and drill-downs to Project and Sprint cards.
Improve cards with larger team avatars, stable titles, and concise status counts.
Enhance Road Map with ongoing-project handling, Project icons, and visible completion context.

sections.title: Dev Tasks and Bug Tracking
items: Unify Dev Task and Bug Tracking tables and dialogs while retaining screen-specific fields.
Add expandable Subtask groups, remembered expansion state, and clearer record metadata.
Automate progress rules across linked Dev Tasks and bugs and highlight bug-linked Dev Tasks.

sections.title: Documentation and Storage
items: Stabilize Documentation cards and allow embedded images to open at native size.
Add headings, numbered lists, strikethrough, and collapsible code blocks to rich-text editing.
Support uploads to local folders and credentialed network file shares.

title: Consistent Sorting and Cleaner Data Views

sections.title: Universal Table Sorting
items: Add ascending, descending, and unsorted column-header states with muted direction indicators.
Keep Subtasks attached to their Parent Task while sorting top-level records.
Synchronize header sorting with filter choices and remember each user's preferred order.

sections.title: View Reset and Wider Coverage
items: Add Reset View controls to Dev Tasks and Bug Tracking.
Extend sorting to Backlog, Scrum, and applicable Settings tables.
Bring Settings filters in line with the Dev Task filter design.

sections.title: Charts, Scrum, and Backlog
items: Remove duplicate browser tooltips and add mouse-wheel scrolling to wide Sprint charts.
Enlarge Scrum avatars and top-align long Scrum entries.
Update Backlog rows to mirror Dev Task and Subtask presentation.

title: Modern Kanban Workflows and Streamlined Planning Views

sections.title: Kanban Board
items: Add explicit read and edit modes so cards open read-only unless editing is enabled.
Add Dev Task-style filtering, Reset View, and synchronized Show All and Hide Empty controls.
Improve card hierarchy with larger avatars, Subtask progress, and cleaner titles and tags.

sections.title: Gantt Chart
items: Introduce a clearer bug icon and remove unnecessary hover outlines.
Move filtering into a Dev Task-style dialog and promote Reset and Fly-By to page actions.
Add bug visibility to Gantt filters while preserving navigation controls.

sections.title: Backlog and Dev Tasks
items: Fix Backlog expand and collapse controls so they remain aligned with avatars.
Add completion information and bring Backlog behavior closer to the Dev Tasks table.
Fix All Projects with Current Sprint filtering so work from every Project is included.

sections.title: Universal Usability
items: Make dialogs draggable while keeping headers reachable on-screen.
Simplify sort labels across filter dialogs.
Optimize large images to improve browser loading without reducing their dimensions.

title: Configurable Work Tables and Bulk Import/Export

sections.title: Configurable Tables
items: Add drag-to-reorder columns in edit mode while keeping hierarchy controls fixed.
Add optional creator, created date, last editor, updated date, and sort-order columns.
Remember each user's column choices and preserve stable sizing for key fields.

sections.title: Faster Filtering
items: Add live Search to filter dialogs with immediate focus.
Improve Sort and Hide Completed placement.
Use the same visual assignee-selection experience as work-item editors.

sections.title: Bug Tracking and Backlog Parity
items: Extend configurable columns, reordering, search, and filtering to Bug Tracking and Backlog.
Add Backlog Reset View and consistent bug-related row accents.
Improve workload-chart avatars so they adapt to available space.

sections.title: CSV and Excel Exchange
items: Add CSV and Excel export and re-import for Dev Tasks, Bug Tracking, and Backlog.
Support bulk updates to titles, progress, status, priority, and assignees.
Continue valid rows when some fail and offer a downloadable error report.

title: Smarter Daily Scrum and Documentation Treeview

sections.title: Guided Scrum Entries
items: Restrict non-admin Scrum dates to valid Project periods while administrators retain unrestricted access.
Carry planned work into the next entry's accomplishments and guide the cursor to today's next action.
Use readable bullet templates and record None when there are no roadblocks.

sections.title: Scrum Table and Filters
items: Move Person to the first column and display names beneath avatars.
Add Search, configurable columns, sorting, import, export, edit mode, reordering, and Reset View.
Restrict normal users to importing updates for their own records.

sections.title: Documentation Treeview
items: Add hierarchical Documents with optional parents, Projects, Sprints, and a Global location.
Introduce a File Explorer-style tree with search, sorting, and flat or hierarchical layouts.
Add independently scrollable panes, a resizable splitter, and in-place viewing and editing.

sections.title: Consistent Page Actions
items: Consolidate secondary actions into consistent overflow menus.
Unify page, navigation, and avatar menus with common icons and dismissal behavior.
Add an edit-mode indicator and make page titles honor renamed navigation labels.

title: Flexible Dialogs, Document Export, and Connected Content

sections.title: Flexible Dialogs and Editors
items: Add maximize, restore, resize, drag, and Reset controls to applicable dialogs.
Reflow fields as dialogs grow and remember dialog dimensions and positions.
Keep rich-text toolbars visible while editing long content.

sections.title: Scrum Improvements
items: Fix Scrum incorrectly opening in edit mode.
Allow users to open and edit their own eligible Scrum entries.
Correct fields that grew vertically when dialogs were resized.

sections.title: Documentation Export
items: Export Documents to Word, self-contained HTML, zipped HTML with images, and browser Print Preview.
Preserve high-resolution images and PMT metadata for future imports.
Make export available from Cards and Treeview.

sections.title: Connected Work Items and Documents
items: Convert Dev Tasks and Bug Reports into Documents.
Carry title, content, Project, and Sprint into the new Document.
Add links in both directions between the Document and its source work item.

title: Personal Logs, Shareable Links, and Full-Page Documentation

sections.title: Personal Log
items: Add a private Log workspace limited to the signed-in user.
Remove Scrum-specific templates and date restrictions from personal logs.
Display subtle created and last-edited timestamps.

sections.title: Linked Bugs and Dev Tasks
items: Copy a bug's rich description into the Dev Task created for its developer.
Synchronize linked Bug and Dev Task status and completion at Ready for QA and QA Passed.
Keep screenshots and rich content available in the linked work.

sections.title: Rich Images and Direct Links
items: Open rich-text images at full resolution from read-only views.
Add image Zoom, Resize, and percentage-based Scale actions.
Update browser URLs during navigation so PMT screens and records can be linked directly.

sections.title: Documentation Workspace
items: Add consistent Cards and Treeview switching with a clear active state.
Add full-width in-place viewing, creation, and editing with sticky controls.
Correct overlapping toolbars, duplicate scrollbars, and filter-reset issues.

title: Portable Content, Private Documentation, and Smarter Imports

sections.title: Documentation Import and Privacy
items: Add validated HTML and Word-compatible document import with clear error feedback.
Update owned documents safely, preserve audit history, and create a new Document when replacement is unsafe.
Add Private and Pinned controls, visibility filters, lock indicators, and pinned-first ordering.

sections.title: Flexible Work-Item Import and Export
items: Add import and export workflows to Dev Tasks, Bug Tracking, and Backlog.
Resolve imported references by ID, name, or sensible current-user and Project defaults.
Show import results with links to imported items and a downloadable review record.

sections.title: Rich Text Editing
items: Add source editing, fonts, sizes, colors, alignment, indentation, and clear formatting.
Organize editing controls into a compact two-row toolbar.
Extract embedded imported images into uploaded files so Documents remain lightweight.

sections.title: Workflow and Deployment Enhancements
items: Preview uploaded avatars during User creation and start new Sprints with all members selected.
Preserve selected Status and completion on new Dev Tasks and keep blank Assignee cells blank.
Support PMT at a domain root or under an IIS application path.

title: Better Rich-Text Colors and Source Editing

sections.title: Office-Style Color Selection
items: Rework font and background color pickers into an easy-to-scan theme palette.
Add custom RGB and HEX entry and remember the user's ten most recent colors.
Correct missing colors, clipped palettes, and inconsistent picker presentation.

sections.title: Source-Code Editing Tools
items: Add word wrapping to the View Source dialog.
Add Copy to Clipboard for quickly reusing or reviewing Document HTML.

sections.title: Documentation Form Alignment
items: Position Pinned beside Private in Card dialogs and the Treeview editor for a consistent layout.

title: Advanced Rich Text, Reliable Kanban, and Better Workflows

sections.title: Rich Text Tables and Checklists
items: Add table insertion, row and column editing, cell colors, and Tab-key navigation.
Add shared interactive checklist items that save without losing scroll position.
Add horizontal dividers, a checklist shortcut, and reliable Undo behavior.

sections.title: Editor Productivity and Media
items: Add dialog-wide toolbar collapse with remembered preferences and last-used color controls.
Store pasted draw.io SVG diagrams as uploaded files rather than oversized embedded HTML.
Expand image actions with Select, Resize, Open in New Tab, and Delete.

sections.title: Kanban and Workflow Reliability
items: Add person filtering and richer Subtask labels.
Prevent a dragged card from opening in Edge and preserve horizontal scroll after moves.
Keep QA Passed at 100% and advance a Parent Task when a Subtask begins.

sections.title: Bug and Export Improvements
items: Default new bugs to Low priority and Minor severity and improve Bug field order.
Use consistent 0–100% completion dropdowns in 5% increments.
Let users include or omit PMT metadata from exports.

title: Custom Work-Item Views, TSG Reporting, and the 3D About Experience

sections.title: Customizable Bug and Dev Task Dialogs
items: Let each user reorder, hide, show, and relabel Bug and Dev Task fields.
Keep read-only and edit layouts synchronized while retaining original field names for reference.
Move secondary dialog actions into a labeled overflow menu.

sections.title: Root Cause Analysis and TSG Reporting
items: Add a rich-text Root Cause Analysis field to Bug Reports.
Add a separately configurable TSG Report workflow based on the user's Bug layout.
Export timestamped TSG reports to CSV and native Excel.

sections.title: Rich-Text and Documentation Stability
items: Replace the image-action dialog with an in-context menu and reliable image actions.
Reduce Documentation card height and prevent footer or content overlap.
Support wheel scrolling inside overflowing card content without shifting centered layouts.

sections.title: About Page in 3D
items: Introduce a full-content 3D About experience centered on an extruded PMT logo.
Add 3D Dev Task and Bug charts, team cards, lighting, stars, UFO events, and camera controls.
Refine gallery layout and camera movement for smooth, readable flybys.

title: PMT's Interactive 3D Gallery

sections.title: Cinematic Gallery Tour
items: Rebuild the flyby as a smooth loop through the PMT logo and major PMT work areas.
Add destination-aware framing, calm curved transitions, and better chart zoom.
Correct abrupt speed changes, rough arrivals, and visual collisions.

sections.title: Live PMT Content in 3D
items: Add labeled 3D areas for Dev Tasks, Bug Tracking, Documentation, Kanban, and the Development Team.
Render recent Documents and a Kanban Board modeled after the normal PMT screen.
Keep chart panels permanently dark for legibility in the space scene.

sections.title: Manual Controls and Preferences
items: Add mouse and keyboard flight controls, pause, restart, speed, and vertical movement.
Add compact control hints and shortcuts for visual and alien events.
Remember About-scene preferences locally.

sections.title: Alien Events and Picture-in-Picture
items: Add comet, lightning, UFO, and multi-ship battle events without disrupting the flyby.
Show picture-in-picture when action is outside the main camera view.
Improve battle dialogue, ship exits, lightning reactions, and event reliability.

title: Invitations, Role-Based Security, and Safer Administration

sections.title: User Invitations and Onboarding
items: Add Invite Users with required Project selection, invite-link generation, and Copy URL.
Add invited-user profile setup with Username suggestions, password, avatar, and Project-aware navigation.
Generate a professional PMT invitation email body for Outlook.

sections.title: Roles and Security Management
items: Add common software-team roles and detailed Read, Create, Update, Delete, Import, Export, and No Access rights.
Support inherited role permissions, per-user overrides, and reset-to-inherited behavior.
Keep administrators unrestricted while making No Access an explicit denial for non-admin users.

sections.title: Security Audit and Account Recovery
items: Add a user-by-resource effective-permissions audit with Excel export.
Add administrator password reset actions for users who cannot sign in.
Show clear warnings before breaking permission inheritance.

sections.title: Attachments and Read-Only Details
items: Add confirmed deletion of existing attachments while editing.
Hide empty fields from read-only dialogs so details focus on actual information.

title: Stronger Privacy, Maintenance Tools, and Direct Links

sections.title: Security and Ownership Fixes
items: Restore sensible default permissions and add a confirmed Reset Security action.
Refine the Security Audit and enforce correct Scrum ownership for non-admin users.
Keep personal Logs and private content private in the UI, including from administrators.

sections.title: Direct URLs and Project Codes
items: Add shareable URLs for PMT screens and Settings categories.
Honor valid preferred Project Codes and provide administrator override support when needed.

sections.title: Administration and Reliability
items: Add Maintenance tools for permanently purging selected soft-deleted records.
Identify, preview, and safely remove orphaned uploaded files.
Allow PMT to load with a clear warning when upload storage is unavailable.

sections.title: About and Screen Saver
items: Restore About credits and repository information and display the PMT database version.
Add an optional idle 3D flyby screen saver that returns users to their exact prior state.
Refine logo flyby clearance and alien-event camera timing.

title: Scrum Attendance Calendar, Collaboration Safety, and Admin Impersonation

sections.title: Daily Scrum Attendance
items: Add Check-In statuses for Home, Office, Sick Leave, Vacation, Emergency Leave, and Other.
Show each known daily status on the team member's avatar and synchronize avatar filtering with Scrum filters.
Allow attendance to be recorded on behalf of another employee.

sections.title: Calendar and Vacation Planning
items: Add a navigable monthly Attendance Calendar above the Scrum table.
Group team avatars by daily status, support multiple statuses, and show holidays when space permits.
Create, edit, cancel, and visualize planned vacations in advance.

sections.title: Collaboration and Form Safety
items: Prevent an older edit from overwriting newer saved work and offer to save conflicting work as a new item.
Fix non-admin QA users' ability to pin their own Scrum entries.
Add stable required-field indicators and reserve space for dynamic Username feedback.

sections.title: Administration and Data Consistency
items: Add administrator impersonation with the target user's permissions, a prominent exit banner, preference restoration, and audit recording.
Add a system-wide Audit Trail in Settings.
Make seeded Documentation public and propagate renamed Project Codes through related Sprints and Tasks.

title: Release Notes, What's New, and Refined Team Workflows

sections.title: Linked Dev Task and Bug Behavior
items: Make Root Cause Analysis a one-way developer-owned flow from a linked Dev Task to its Bug Report.
Copy a Bug Report URL into the linked Dev Task when work is assigned.
Allow Developers to move Kanban work through QA Passed, but not into later deployment statuses.

sections.title: Scrum View Refinements
items: Enlarge daily-status avatars, center them in the title area, and prevent loaded status avatars from shifting content.
Increase status-overlay visibility and replace the ambiguous Sick icon.
Center Table and Calendar view buttons using the same presentation as Documentation views while keeping the calendar above the Scrum table.
Automatically reduce daily-status avatar sizes when needed so they stay between the page title and the view buttons without collisions.
Refresh visible Scrum entries, daily attendance avatars, and Calendar attendance or vacation changes every five seconds while Scrum is active.
Provide one default-on Auto Refresh setting for Table and Calendar that pauses during active editing and preserves the current view, filters, focus, menus, and scroll position.

sections.title: User Profiles and Administration
items: Show each user's most recent successful login on their Settings card.
Preserve an administrator's configured role or title and identify their elevated access with an Admin suffix.

sections.title: Team Mentions
items: Recognize @Sin-style references in Release Notes and rendered rich-text content across PMT.
Reveal a compact User Card when a reader hovers over or focuses a team mention, helping new team members identify colleagues.

sections.title: Release Notes
items: Add a Release Notes screen with polished business-facing notes and the original Sin's AI Prompt Engineering.
Organize daily releases from newest to oldest with navigation between releases.
Group features, enhancements, and bug fixes into clear sections.
Keep the page title and view controls visible while reading long notes and align release navigation with the content area.

sections.title: What's New
items: Show releases published since the user's previous login in a What's New dialog.
Provide navigation for multiple unread releases and limit first-time users to the latest three.
Link to the full Release Notes screen and reuse the same release-note content for both experiences.
Notify signed-in users once when a same-day release is revised, without replaying unrelated older notes.

sections.title: Repeatable PMT Demonstrations
items: Preserve BDO's working PMT Project as PMTQA while restoring a separate copy of the original SQL-seeded PMT Project for demonstrations.
Allow administrators to clear and restore the PMT demo repeatedly without changing PMTQA, BDO users, permissions, or private content.
Safely recreate missing demo team profiles and keep seeded Bug values aligned with the active BDO lookup configuration.
Seed planned vacations for shared PMT, LMS, and HLS demo team members so the Scrum Attendance Calendar immediately demonstrates planned leave.

sections.title: Routed Dialog and Scrum Refinements
items: Restore the screen URL when a routed read-only item dialog closes.
Default Scrum attendance to Office, remember each user's selection, and allow On Behalf Of entries for a selected date.
Align the Scrum page title and action controls with Dev Tasks and let Calendar users remove attendance directly from an avatar action menu.

sections.title: Safer Development Resets
items: Clarify the Development screen warnings, reset descriptions, and destructive button labels.
Allow administrators to clear every Project except PMT or factory-reset the database to the original demo Projects.
Let intentional Development reset operations proceed even when private content exists.

sections.title: Faster Planning Screen Filters
items: Standardize synchronized Project, Sprint, and Search controls across Dev Tasks, Bug Tracking, Backlog, Kanban Board, Documentation, and Sprints.
Add multi-item deletion in edit mode and keep page actions fixed while header controls compact.
Keep active searches visibly expanded in their chosen position, update results after a short typing pause, and render idle Project and Sprint context as compact baseline-aligned labels.
Extend the responsive Project and Search header, smooth background filtering, and multi-item deletion to each user's private Log.
Simplify Documentation's New and Filter actions to icon-only controls while retaining accessible names and tooltips.

title: Editable Image Annotation in Rich Text

sections.title: Full-Resolution Image Editing
items: Annotate pasted rich-text images in a dedicated full-screen workspace without leaving the record being edited.
Keep the original full-resolution image available while saving a portable SVG that can be reopened for editing.
Crop to the important part of a screenshot and restore the full source image whenever needed.

Work on a large centered pasteboard that expands when an image or annotation is dropped past its edge, place objects freely beyond the image, and automatically trim unused workspace from the SVG applied to rich text.

sections.title: Diagram and Formatting Tools
items: Add and resize rectangles, arrows, and wrapped text boxes, with configurable fills, outlines, fonts, sizes, colors, left/center/right horizontal text alignment, and Top/Middle/Bottom vertical text alignment.
Center text boxes visually from the font's actual ascent and descent so Middle alignment balances the visible text instead of its baseline box.
Use recognizable glyph buttons for Select, Crop, Rectangle, Arrow, and Text Box, with keyboard shortcuts and accessible names.

Select arrows by their painted shaft and head, drag either endpoint to adjust length and rotation without distorting line width or arrowhead size, and move the whole arrow by dragging or using the keyboard.

Keep images, shapes, text boxes, and multi-object groups proportional while resizing, hold Ctrl to resize normal objects or groups symmetrically from their center, and hold Alt to temporarily resize a standalone rectangle freely with its grabbed handle tracking the snapped pointer.

Keep a singly selected arrow's opposite endpoint fixed and ignore Ctrl during arrow endpoint editing or dragging, while arrows inside a multi-object group proportionally scale their length, shaft width, and arrowhead size with the group.

Use a PMT-themed Format pane with the familiar rich-text Font Color and Background Color palettes, plus six visibly filled shared recent-color swatches beside Fill, Outline color, and Text color in compact three-by-two strips, and hide or show the pane without changing the zoom or centered work area.
Show or hide each rectangle or text-box outline independently without losing its chosen color, with checked-by-default visibility preserved through undo, redo, save, and reopen.

Adjust rectangle, arrow, and text-box opacity from 0 to 100 percent without changing source or embedded images, and preserve each vector's opacity through templates, clipboard operations, export, Apply, and reopen.

Right-click a selection for a familiar PMT-styled menu ordered Crop, To Front, To Back, Forward, Backward, separator, Group, Ungroup, Reset Crop, Lock or Unlock, separator, Copy as SVG, and Copy as Image, while dotted guides identify every selected group member, including base-to-tip guides for arrows.
Copy the current object or selected group as a tightly cropped, self-contained vector SVG or PNG image while preserving crop, fill, line, text, and outline-visibility styling.
Honor saved group membership immediately when reopening an annotation, including when the source image belongs to the group.
Use grid snapping for precise placement and return keyboard focus to the last selected object after changing Grid or Snap.
Return to Select after drawing each object, marquee-select every object touched from blank canvas, and clear the selection by clicking outside it.

sections.title: Confident Editing
items: Undo and redo annotation changes from the toolbar or keyboard shortcuts.
Delete selected annotations, press Escape to clear a selection, and nudge objects by the visible grid interval or one pixel when the grid is hidden.
Press Ctrl+A while working on the canvas to select every annotation object without taking native select-all behavior away from formatting or text fields.
Scroll the workspace, zoom around the pointer, and pan large screenshots with the middle mouse button.
Maximize the annotation editor to the true browser viewport, use Cancel or Apply to RTE from its top toolbar, and restore the normal dialog without losing the current zoom or centered work area.
Move or resize the source image without clipping it to its former position; intentional crop boundaries travel and scale with the image.
Keep both the editable SVG and original image attached to the saved rich-text record so normal Maintenance reference protection applies.

sections.title: Reusable Templates and Faster Canvas Editing
items: Switch between Format and Template tabs, save up to 50 large-preview templates, and rename, update, reorder, or delete them independently of applying or canceling the current canvas.
Keep each user's template library and Arrow or Rectangle drawing defaults with their PMT account so the same tools are available after signing in from another browser or computer.

Capture company logos together with rectangles, arrows, and text as native grouped templates, preserving the original image bytes and editable vector members without creating orphan-prone upload files.

Insert a detached template instance with fresh object identities, ungroup and edit its members normally, and leave existing canvas instances unchanged when their source template is later updated or deleted.
Copy and paste selected canvas objects with Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V, duplicate them with Ctrl+D and a confirmation toast, and preserve normal shortcut behavior inside text and formatting controls.

Hold Shift for horizontal-only dragging or Alt for vertical-only dragging, hide or show the Right Pane from the toolbar, and use Fit to tightly center only the painted image and vector content in the available viewport.

sections.title: Compact Object Tree and Layer Control
items: Switch to a compact Objects tab whose top-to-bottom rows are the actual SVG paint stack, from the highest/frontmost item to the lowest/backmost item, while flat logical groups show their children and the original source image stays fixed at the bottom layer.
Filter object and group names case-insensitively without changing selection or z-order, keeping a matched child's group parent visible and every child visible when its group matches.
Rename object and group nodes, multi-select and delete eligible items, and copy or paste native objects directly from the tree while keeping tree and canvas selection synchronized.

Drag standalone or grouped rows to repaint the canvas immediately in the matching SVG z-order, move objects between the root and logical groups, and merge one flat group into another without creating nested groups.
Preserve custom object and group names, tree order, grouping, and all tree-driven edits through undo, redo, Apply, and reopening the editable annotation.

title: Shared Annotation Templates and Entity-Relationship Diagrams

sections.title: Default Annotation Templates
items: Make the complete 13-template PMT annotation library available by default to every user on existing and newly created Projects.
Add a non-destructive Restore Default Templates action that places only missing defaults at the top while preserving each user's personal templates and drawing defaults.
Recognize matching template designs even when their internal identities differ, and leave customized personal versions untouched.

sections.title: Selection-Aware Template Formatting
items: Insert a detached template instance only when the canvas has no current selection; otherwise apply the template's formatting to the selected objects.
Map matching structures one-to-one, and warn before using best-effort type matching when the template and destination structures differ.
Preserve destination text, geometry, identities, grouping, lock state, and layer order while applying formatting, with the complete change available through one Undo or Redo operation.

sections.title: Confident Layer Reordering
items: Show one horizontal insertion line while dragging annotation objects or groups so the destination layer is clear before dropping.
Allow an object or group to be placed above the current top group by dropping on its upper edge without accidentally joining that group.
Keep lower-edge group drops available for intentionally moving objects into an existing group.

sections.title: Documentation Header Polish
items: Keep the Project and Sprint dropdown top borders fully visible in Documentation Tree View, matching Card View.

sections.title: Dedicated Diagram Workspace
items: Add a top-level Diagram workspace immediately after Documentation while reusing the same annotation editor available from rich-text content.
Insert Rectangle, Arrow, Text, and Entity objects immediately from the toolbar, start with Grid and Snap disabled, and provide direct 10% through 200% zoom selection in 5% increments.
Create a private backing Document immediately for every new Diagram, then edit the same saved Diagram inline or in a maximized workspace.
Browse Diagrams in Card or hierarchical Tree views, edit their name, visibility, Project, Sprint, parent, and pinned state, and share direct links to public Diagrams.
Filter Diagrams by search, Project, Sprint, and visibility; sort latest-first, oldest-first, alphabetically, or by a custom drag-and-drop order while keeping pinned Diagrams at the top.
Navigate read-only Diagrams with Fit, direct zoom selection, pointer-centered zoom, and drag panning across a virtual canvas even when the artwork already fits the viewport.

sections.title: SQL Server Entity Modeling
items: Create Entity objects from SQL Server CREATE TABLE scripts or simple field lists while preserving schema, field names, data types, identity, nullability, primary keys, and foreign keys.
Edit and reorder fields, manually designate Primary Key, Foreign Key, or Important fields, and map foreign keys to their referenced Entity fields and relationship cardinality.
Collapse Entities to key fields, move foreign keys below primary keys, and toggle compact data-type columns directly from each Entity header or its Entity tab.

sections.title: Readable Relationship Diagrams
items: Connect every relationship from its dependent field to the exact referenced source field, with the arrowhead pointing toward the source and the selected cardinality rendered at the endpoints.
Auto-format Entities as an org-tree hierarchy with the selected central table on top, direct children aligned together, movable descendants, and optional Anchor tables that remain fixed.
Route relationship lines around Entity bounds, keep overlapping segments visually consistent, and hide self-referencing connectors by default unless an Entity enables them.

sections.title: Reusable ERD Styling
items: Format all Entity relationships through their shared Objects container or select an individual relationship to change only its line color, line width, and arrow-head size.
Save relationship formatting as a reusable template and download or upload individual template files, including the ten key PMT Entity templates created for ERD testing.
Resize objects proportionally from corner handles or along one axis from side handles while keeping Entity key columns stable and scaling embedded text or arrows proportionally.
Hide or show any individual canvas object or complete object group from the Objects tab, with visibility preserved through Undo, Redo, templates, saving, and reopening.

sections.title: Permanent PMT Database Schema Demo
items: Seed a public PMT's Database Schema Diagram that models every PMT application table and its real field-to-field foreign-key relationships.
Arrange the schema on a large editable canvas beneath the title PMT's Diagram Tool by Sin so the Diagram workspace has a durable, realistic demonstration after fresh installation or migration.
Group the per-entity explanatory text and pointing arrows into one Entity Descriptions overlay that can be shown or hidden without affecting the underlying ERD.

title: Faster Diagrams, Flexible Cropping, and Live Database Schemas

sections.title: Unified Images and Cropping
items: Treat Original Image as a normal editable image layer on the shared annotation and Diagram canvas instead of a special fixed background.
Mark cropped images in the Objects tree and let users temporarily show or hide each saved crop without removing it.

Offer Remove Crop or Apply Crop Permanently when cropping an already-cropped image, with an irreversible-action warning and normal Maintenance orphan detection for the replaced source after the record is saved.
Upload pasted PNG and SVG images to PMT storage from both rich-text and Diagram canvases, then keep the saved content URL-backed instead of embedding base64 image data.
Include pasted source images together with arrows and other annotations when copying a Diagram selection as PNG or SVG.

sections.title: Diagrams in Rich Text
items: Add Insert Diagram to rich-text editors so users can create an editable diagram at the current caret without starting from an image.
Keep image annotations and image-free diagrams on the same canvas, object, history, formatting, and reopen behavior.

sections.title: Readable Source and Code Blocks
items: Use Consolas in View Source and enable its shortened Word Wrap option by default.
Choose None, C#, T-SQL, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, or Java from the Code Block dialog, with each language change reapplied from the plain source instead of stacking formatting.
Format and color valid JSON, keep invalid or unsupported content as plain text with a helpful notification, and render inserted code blocks collapsed by default in both edit and read-only views.

sections.title: Faster and Cleaner Diagram Navigation
items: Make edit-mode zooming substantially more responsive on large entity-relationship diagrams while preserving pointer-centered zoom and fast panning.
Smooth pointer-centered wheel zoom in both edit and read-only modes with lightweight vector transforms, then settle the canvas once after each gesture instead of rebuilding it on every frame.

Keep oversized SVG canvases within the browser's safe rendering budget so minimum zoom stays fully painted and Entity borders, relationship lines, and symbols scale together without flicker, thick lines, or incomplete rendering.
Highlight and drag visible Entities without rerendering the full schema on every pointer movement, keeping interaction responsive on large diagrams.
Scale relationship rendering for schemas with hundreds of tables by reusing route geometry, checking nearby obstacles, and painting shared same-style line segments once.
Return a cancelled Diagram edit to a fitted, centered Tree view and keep read-only zooming responsive.
Match edit mode in read-only Diagrams by hiding each Entity's expand, collapse, and Data Type controls until the pointer is over or near that Entity.
Use the expand, collapse, and Data Type controls directly in read-only Diagrams without losing the current view position.

sections.title: More Expressive and Readable ERDs
items: Keep arrows and relationship symbols off by default, control them from synchronized global switches, and temporarily turn off every relationship line when users want an Entity-only view.
Add an Entity Annotation field that creates a linked, styleable callout and arrow which follow the Entity as it moves and persist with the Diagram.
Style each Entity name and header background independently to highlight important tables.

Route relationships around unrelated Entities and Entity Annotation callouts with visible clearance, and move non-anchored tables away from relationship lines during Org Tree formatting while leaving Anchor tables fixed.
Keep every Entity relationship field-to-field: connectors attach only to their mapped PK or FK rows and never terminate on or brush another part of an Entity.

sections.title: Live PMT Database Schema
items: Generate a new private PMT's Database Schema Diagram from the connected database's current tables, columns, keys, relationships, and data types without replacing the Diagram being edited.
Protect live schema generation with Documentation/Create permission and deliver its stored procedure through the Version 1.22-to-1.23 forward migration and operator verification runbook.

sections.title: Polished Diagram Workspace
items: Keep Diagram title, visibility, Project and Sprint details, editing actions, zoom, and Fit together in the page header; show private locks in the tree and use compact icon-only New Diagram and Filters actions.
Use a checkable Left Nav menu, always reveal the Left Nav when Treeview is chosen, resize the right inspector, and automatically fit and center each Diagram selected from the tree.
Create new Diagrams directly in the full-screen editor at 100% zoom, start with the Green Box with Text template centered and selected, and support zoom levels through 300%.
Double-click a text box to open Format, bring its Text field into view, focus it, and select existing text for immediate replacement.

Show lock cues for locked or anchored Objects, temporarily unanchor or re-anchor every Entity from one global control, and suppress Anchor Table Shortcut warnings while every Entity is globally unanchored.
When another edit wins a save conflict, preserve that newer version and offer to save the user's work as a newly numbered Diagram instead of discarding it.
Search both Diagram metadata and SVG contents, and use matching right-click actions for Edit Info, Edit Diagram, Download, and Delete while public-link sharing and pinning are temporarily hidden.

Add Circle and plain Line objects to the toolbar, with new Circles starting at equal width and height, Lines following Arrow editing behavior without an arrowhead, and a clearer outlined-square icon for Outline color.
Keep Entity headers, fields, and data types visible while tables are resized or dragged in edit mode.
Copy selected objects or the full Diagram as lean, self-contained SVG, preserving logical groups as standard SVG groups without PMT editor metadata or controls.
Fit an existing Diagram to the available editor space as soon as editing opens, while new Diagrams retain their 100% starting zoom.

sections.title: Focused Documentation Editing
items: Use one checked Left Nav item in Documentation's overflow menu, and open new Documents or card edits in the full-screen editor with the Left Nav hidden.
Keep View Full-Screen available after entering edit mode from a Documentation card.
Show compact Created by and Last Edited by names and timestamps beside Project and Sprint details in Treeview.
Match Diagram Treeview with document right-click actions and drag-and-drop hierarchy ordering while document pinning is temporarily hidden.

sections.title: Streamlined Scrum
items: Keep the attendance table visible, hide the optional Calendar by default, and toggle the Calendar from the overflow menu without replacing the table.
Choose a Check-In status from a focused dialog with Office first and selected by default instead of using a separate Attendance dropdown.
Align 80-pixel checked-in avatars and Scrum content with other PMT screens without a clipped hover outline, and click the same avatar again to clear its user filter.
Prefill a new Scrum's Yesterday list from that user's newest-created Scrum in the selected Project, including entries dated today, and leave it blank when the Project has no prior entry.

sections.title: Consistent Light Theme and Compact Kanban
items: Temporarily hide Dark Theme and consistently restore Light Theme while the alternate theme is reviewed.
Keep Subtask labels, percentages, and progress bars contained inside narrow Kanban cards on 720p displays.

sections.title: More Noticeable What's New
items: Show a lightweight Diagram Tool illustration above this What's New entry using simple tables and straight connector lines without connector symbols.

title: Diagram ERD Routing, Rich Code Blocks, and Release Hardening

sections.title: ERD Relationship Routing
items: Route Entity relationships field-to-field only, choosing short local paths between PK/FK fields instead of touching a table body or taking long perimeter routes.
Treat each Entity's visual margin as a protected no-cross zone, with relationship lines entering that margin only for the short horizontal connection into or out of a field.
Keep relationship trunks compact around tables, avoid excessive fan-out, reserve extra space above table headers, and keep passing lines away from unrelated Entities.

sections.title: Compact Diagram Layout
items: Rename the ERD layout action to Auto Format - Compact and use Sin's compact style to pull unanchored tables back toward balanced left and right corridors.
Prevent Entity overlaps in both read-only and edit modes, cascading movable tables aside after loading, dragging, resizing, pasting, duplicating, or expanding Data Types.
Bring an Entity forward when its controls are used and keep expanded tables visible instead of letting neighboring Entities cover their headers or fields.

sections.title: Diagram Editing Tools
items: Add circle and plain-line drawing tools, a reusable Format Painter, live color hover previews, cleaner SVG copy output, and fit-to-screen behavior when editing existing Diagrams.
Let selected relationship segments expose lightweight handles for manual path adjustment and provide a clearer selected-line indicator plus a way to clear manual paths.

Create Entity Annotations from a dialog instead of a live text field, support optional annotation arrows, group Entity callouts with invisible group behavior, and edit text boxes from a dialog on double-click.

Duplicate Diagrams as independent copies, search SVG contents in Diagram filters, show Creator and Last Edited filter sections, and keep Diagram info metadata visible with created and last-modified user timestamps.

sections.title: Documentation and Header Layout
items: Duplicate Documentation entries as independent copies, including copied rich-text images and best-effort copied attachments.
Align Project, Sprint, and Search controls across Documentation, Kanban, Backlog, Bugs, Sprints, Log, and Dev Task headers on narrower screens.
Tighten the Diagram Treeview content area, remove the extra outer frame around the read-only canvas, and give the Diagram more usable workspace.

sections.title: Rich Text Code Blocks
items: Add copy, edit, and delete actions to rich-text Code Blocks while preserving undo when a block is removed.
Stabilize the Code Block editor so pasted multi-line code keeps its formatting, scrollbars, syntax highlighting, caption editing, and collapse indicator.

sections.title: Exports, Preferences, and Release Readiness
items: Export Dev Tasks and Bug Reports with pasted rich-text images embedded correctly and without PMT metadata in the exported HTML.
Clear browser-stored user preferences from Development by logging the user out so they can start from a clean local state.
Rename the prompt-history view to Sin's AI Prompt Engineering, format the original prompts with headings and numbered lists, and show daily and running prompt statistics.
Record the BDO deployment milestone as the new database baseline and keep combined migration runners as the deployment path for future database changes.

title: Diagram, RTE, and Documentation Usability

sections.title: Diagram Authoring and ERD Workflow
items: Add PMT Diagram export/import support so editable diagrams can move between PMT instances using a versioned PMT Diagram file format.

Improve Entity editing with better PK/FK/Important alignment, clickable Important headers, dropdown-based FK mapping, schema-aware template names, alphabetical field ordering, and manual connector nudging.
Teach Auto Format - Compact to honor the preferred compact ERD connector routing rules so related relationship lines share clean local lanes instead of fanning out into long perimeter routes.
Add a Rich Text Editor canvas object for tutorial-style Diagrams, letting users place formatted content and color-coded code directly on a Diagram.
Make relationship lines clickable in read-only Diagram viewers so users can select one connector at a time and visually trace complex ERDs.

sections.title: Rich Text, Logs, Scrum, Dev Tasks, and Bugs
items: Add general RTE expand/collapse sections, compact code-block headers, default-collapsed code blocks with an override, rich color-aware code copying, code preview, indentation trimming, maximize support, and reliable code-block deletion.
Stabilize linked Diagram viewers embedded in Documentation and Scrum rich text so they stay hydrated after read-only rendering and interaction.
Fix Logs read-mode code-block clicks, update the default Logs heading to Personal Logs, correct the New Log dialog layout, and move the RTE code-block toolbar button after Clear Formatting.
Make Dev Task and Bug Tracking new/edit dialogs use the same full-screen experience, including auto full-screen for new records.

sections.title: Documentation, Navigation, and What's New
items: Make Documentation and Diagram TreeView toggles hide or show their left navigation panes without switching away from TreeView.
Show private Documentation items in the TreeView with the same lock icon style used by Diagram.
Refresh the Day 34 What's New artwork with a clean, corporate-safe people, technology, and teams illustration.
Regenerate the Day 34 Release Notes and What's New data from the current Requirements file so the latest prompt history and summaries stay synchronized.
